channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Late Night Wonders Which Former President Spoke With Trump
item.description: In guessing which ex-leader Trump might have discussed Iran with, Jimmy Fallon said “two things seem equally possible: Either Trump’s lying, or Joe Biden doesn’t remember talking to him.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Homelessness, Benefit Cuts, Budget Woes: A New Commissioner’s Challenges
item.description: Erin Dalton, who is starting her job as commissioner of social services under Mayor Zohran Mamdani, says she’ll be tackling some of the country’s toughest problems.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth A. Harris

media:content.media:credit: Sara Naomi Lewkowicz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Erin Dalton, the new commissioner of social services, will oversee a budget of more than $18 billion.

item.title: March Madness
item.description: Will you be watching? What predictions can you make?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: An Army Reservist Owns a Cannabis Dispensary. It May End His Career.
item.description: Selling marijuana is strictly prohibited in the military. What that means for soldiers who own businesses is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: James Estrin/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: William Norgard in his Manhattan apartment. He is a reserve officer who has been reprimanded by the Army for his role in owning a state-licensed cannabis dispensary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 18, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Los cubanoestadounidenses podrán tener negocios en Cuba, pero ¿es suficiente?
item.description: Expertos señalaron que los cubanoestadounidenses que huyeron lo hicieron porque se les confiscaron sus negocios y difícilmente podrían regresar a Cuba sin garantías jurídicas básicas.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:credit: Yamil Lage/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Un par de personas frente a una tienda en La Habana el lunes

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Missiles Hit Israel as Iran Responds to Latest Killings
item.description: Two people were killed by missile strikes in the Israeli city of Ramat Gan, officials said, as Tehran retaliated for the killing of its de facto leader and a militia commander. Heavy airstrikes were also reported in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: Qué significa enlighten
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 17 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Predije la crisis financiera de 2008. Lo que se avecina puede ser peor
item.description: Estamos en un periodo de riesgo, plagado de presiones como las que han provocado grandes crisis financieras. Esta vez, los riesgos se extienden a través de industrias, mercados y naciones.
item.dc:creator: Richard Bookstaber

item.title: John Galliano lanzará colecciones con Zara
item.description: El diseñador se embarca en una colaboración con Zara.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Jacopo Raule/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: John Galliano en una fiesta de Vogue World en París en junio de 2024.

item.title: Word of the Day: enlighten
item.description: This word has appeared in 17 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Mixed Results for AIPAC and Allies in Illinois House Primaries
item.description: Organizations linked to the pro-Israel lobbying organization had some victories and some less-than-ideal outcomes in closely watched Democratic contests.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Jim Vondruska for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The American Israel Public Affairs Committee took credit on social media for its political maneuvering, suggesting it that contributed to Kat Abughazaleh’s loss.

item.title: Illinois Primary Election Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC’s Power Is Tested
item.description: Democratic voters put Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton on a path to the Senate, while the pro-Israel lobby notched its first major victories of the year but also faced a tough defeat.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton of Illinois won the state’s Democratic primary race for Senate, backed by millions from Gov. JB Pritzker.

item.title: Un ataque aéreo pakistaní dejó decenas de muertos en Kabul
item.description: El ataque impactó un centro de rehabilitación de drogas. Afganistán prometió tomar represalias, lo que eleva el riesgo de una escalada del conflicto entre ambos países.
item.dc:creator: Safiullah Padshah and Elian Peltier

media:content.media:credit: Sayed Hassib/Reuters
media:content.media:description: El ataque aéreo alcanzó un centro de rehabilitación de drogas en Kabul, dijeron funcionarios afganos.

item.title: This Isn’t What Cubans Have Been Fighting For
item.description: Trump wants a symbolic win. Islanders want actual democracy.
item.dc:creator: Maria de los Angeles Torres

media:content.media:credit: Adalberto Roque/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: After a blackout in Havana in March.

item.title: Israel, the Iran War, and the G.O.P.’s Growing Antisemitism Problem
item.description: Michelle Goldberg on a dark political reality she found in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg, Daniel J. Wakin and Derek Arthur

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photograph by Damon Winter/The New York Times

item.title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
item.description: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump unveiled the TrumpRx website last month. Visitors to the site are greeted by a promise: “Find the world’s lowest prices on prescription drugs.”

item.title: The Faroe Islands Are Changing Some of Europe’s Strictest Abortion Rules
item.description: A new law allowing abortion up to 12 weeks will be a major shift in an archipelago of 55,000 people, and there are strong feelings on both sides.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Regin Winther Poulsen

media:content.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Marna Jacobsen, 71, in Torshavn, Faroe Islands, late last year. Ms. Jacobsen said that as a 17-year-old she had to lie to get an abortion.

item.title: Trump’s Friendship With Japan’s Leader Takaichi Faces Test Over Iran
item.description: In Washington on Thursday, President Trump is expected to press Sanae Takaichi for military help in the Strait of Hormuz. But she faces constraints on what she can offer.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan aboard the U.S.S. George Washington at Yokosuka Port south of Yokohama, Japan, in October.

item.title: Netanyahu Hopes Strikes on Iran Will Lead to Uprising and Regime Change
item.description: Israeli attacks have targeted the command centers of Iran’s repressive, internal security forces in hopes that Iranians will overthrow their rulers. Some see that as wishful thinking.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Ronen Bergman and David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran shows Iran’s three supreme leaders.

item.title: As War Chokes Natural Gas Supply, Asia Turns Back to Coal
item.description: Across Asia, a sharp drop in liquefied natural gas supplies is pushing major importers back toward coal, undermining L.N.G.’s long-held role as a stable energy anchor.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:credit: Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Qatar Energy announced a shutdown of its liquefied natural gas production after Iranian attacks on energy installations.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Tech’s Big Bet On Weapons Is Paying Off
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, March 18, 2026.

item.title: La Shawn K. Ford Wins Nomination for House Seat Long Held by Danny Davis
item.description: Mr. Ford, who received Mr. Davis’s endorsement, emerged from a wide field of candidates seeking to represent a district that includes the West Side of Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: Erin Hooley/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: La Shawn K. Ford, a state lawmaker, won the Democratic Party’s nomination.

item.title: Juliana Stratton Wins Illinois Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: Ms. Stratton, the state’s lieutenant governor, prevailed with millions of dollars of help from Gov. JB Pritzker, a billionaire. She will be heavily favored in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton will be on the ballot for Senate in Illinois in November.

item.title: Daniel Biss, Mayor of Evanston, Ill., Wins Democratic Nomination for House Seat
item.description: Mr. Biss emerged after being an early front-runner in the turbulent, crowded contest to replace a longtime incumbent in Illinois’s Ninth District.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Daniel Biss of Evanston, Ill., at Fox Chicago’s debate last month.

item.title: Centrist Melissa Bean Wins 8th District Democratic Primary
item.description: Ms. Bean, a moderate former congresswoman, defeated a left-wing rival in the primary race for the Chicago-area seat to be vacated by Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Evan Vucci/Associated Press

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 18, 2026
item.description: Adam Vincent’s puzzle is a winner.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Toni Preckwinkle, Longtime Chicago Democrat, Holds Off Moderate Challenger
item.description: Ms. Preckwinkle has been president of the Cook County Board for four terms. Brendan Reilly, a member of the Chicago City Council, mounted a challenge to her right in a Democratic primary.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Toni Preckwinkle, president of the Cook County Board of Commissioners, is a four-term incumbent.

item.title: Don Tracy Wins Republican Senate Primary in Illinois
item.description: The former state party chair will begin the general election as a significant underdog in a heavily Democratic state.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Dominic Di Palermo/Chicago Tribune, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Don Tracy, the former chair for the Illinois Republican Party, won the Republican primary for the open Senate seat in Illinois on Tuesday.

item.title: Darren Bailey Wins Second Chance to Challenge Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker
item.description: Mr. Bailey, a former lawmaker aligned with President Trump, lost to Mr. Pritzker in 2022. Mr. Pritzker had no opposition in a Democratic primary.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: E. Jason Wambsgans/Chicago Tribune, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Darren Bailey, a former state lawmaker, is running for governor of Illinois.

item.title: Donna Miller Beats Jesse Jackson Jr. in Illinois House Primary
item.description: Ms. Miller, a Cook County commissioner and a former health care consultant, blocked a comeback attempt from Mr. Jackson, who had served time in federal prison.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Erin Hooley/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Donna Miller cast herself as the candidate of the people.

item.title: U.S.P.S. Postmaster Says Service Will Be ‘Out of Cash’ in Under a Year
item.description: The U.S.P.S. leader said at a hearing that the service could run out of cash within a year and asked lawmakers to increase its borrowing limits.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:credit: Cliff Owen/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Postmaster General David Steiner

item.title: Cuban Americans Will Be Allowed to Own Businesses in Cuba, but Is That Enough to Rescue the Economy?
item.description: The Cuban government announced that Cubans living abroad can own and invest in businesses, but experts said they were disappointed that the measures didn’t go further.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:credit: Yamil Lage/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: People stand outside a store in Havana on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Says Anthropic Is an ‘Unacceptable’ National Security Risk
item.description: In a legal filing, the government said it questioned whether the A.I. start-up could be a “trusted partner” in wartime, which led it to label the company a supply chain risk.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Anthropic has accused the Pentagon of labeling it a “supply chain risk” to punish it on ideological grounds.

item.title: Madrid Travel Guide: What to Do, Eat and Where to Stay
item.description: From the top attractions to the most frequently asked questions, our guide has all you need to plan your next visit.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ferren

media:content.media:credit: Carlos Chavarria for The New York Times

item.title: Judge Orders Voice of America to Restart All News Operations
item.description: More than 1,000 full-time journalists and support staff at the news group were ordered to return to work by March 23 and to resume broadcasting.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Agency for Global Media

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A federal judge’s ruling orders more than 1,000 full-time workers and support staff to return to work at Voice of America by March 23.

item.title: Police Kill Gunman Who Shot Employee at Georgia V.A. Clinic
item.description: The employee’s condition was not immediately disclosed. The Department of Veterans Affairs said the clinic would be closed for the rest of the week, while the authorities investigate.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Emilie Megnien/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A gunman was fatally shot after he opened fire inside the Pickens County V.A. Clinic in Jasper, Ga., on Tuesday, wounding a worker there, the authorities said.

item.title: Una bomba hallada en un campo de Colombia provoca un choque diplomático
item.description: El Times fotografió una munición sin estallar en el sur de Colombia, cerca de la frontera con Ecuador. Rápidamente, se produjo un enfrentamiento entre ambos países.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, John Ismay and Fotografías por Federico Rios

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The remnants of a 500‑pound undetonated bomb dropped by the Ecuadorian army that fell on a coca crop in Colombia.

item.title: Year After Trump Takeover, Institute of Peace Is Little More Than Scenery
item.description: As a lawsuit by the former staff drags on, the fight highlights President Trump’s upending of traditional peace-building and the lasting effects of his administration’s cost-cutting blitz.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Institute of Peace was recently renamed the Donald J. Trump United States Institute of Peace.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The deaths of two top Iranian officials were announced on Tuesday, while President Trump once again lashed out at NATO allies who refused to aid the U.S. war effort against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ali Larijani, center, registering his name as a candidate for presidential elections in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: ‘Ulster American’ Review: Matthew Broderick as a Star Blinded by Privilege
item.description: David Ireland’s satire follows a Hollywood actor whose cluelessness leads to a combustible confrontation.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Papo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: From left, Geraldine Hughes, Matthew Broderick and Max Baker, as a playwright, star and director in “Ulster American,” at the Irish Repertory Theater.

item.title: Counties to Watch in the Illinois Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: There is more to the state than Chicago and its suburbs, even if that area makes up a large majority of the vote.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang

media:content.media:credit: Christine Zhang/The New York Times

item.title: In His Resignation Letter, Joe Kent Spoke About the Death of His Wife
item.description: Chief Petty Officer Shannon Kent was killed in action during a special operations mission in Syria in 2019. Mr. Kent said he could not support “sending the next generation off to fight and die” in Iran.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jenny Kane/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned on Tuesday.

item.title: When to Expect Primary Election Results From Illinois
item.description: Voters kicked off the state’s midterm contests for U.S. Senate, U.S. House and other offices on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters in Illinois will be casting ballots to nominate major party candidates for the U.S. Senate and in all 17 of Illinois’s House districts.

item.title: No Trump Endorsement for Cornyn or Paxton in Texas Senate Race as Deadline Passes
item.description: President Trump’s decision not to weigh in before the deadline means both John Cornyn and Ken Paxton remain on the ballot, extending their costly and increasingly personal battle into a May runoff.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago, Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Next Decision in War: Whether to Retrieve Iran’s Nuclear Fuel
item.description: A mission to seize or destroy Iran’s nuclear material would be one of the riskiest military operations in modern American history.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vantor, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A satellite image of the Pickaxe Mountain tunnel complex near the Natanz nuclear facility in central Iran last week. It is not certain where all of Iran’s nuclear material resides.

item.title: Muere Alfredo Bryce Echenique, novelista peruano, a los 87 años
item.description: En sus galardonados libros, aportó una visión desde dentro a las historias sobre la indiferencia de la élite de su país y el sufrimiento silencioso de las clases más desfavorecidas.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:credit: Raphael Gaillarde/Gamma-Rapho, vía Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Alfredo Bryce Echenique en 1999 en París, donde vivió durante mucho tiempo en un exilio autoimpuesto. “Solía decir: ‘Los elitistas son profundamente ignorantes’”, dijo su biógrafo.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Raja Krishnamoorthi, Democratic Senate Hopeful in Illinois
item.description: The congressman, an Ivy League-educated lawyer known for his fund-raising prowess, is aiming to become the second Indian American elected to the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Across five terms in Congress, Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi has earned a reputation as an ambitious political operator.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Juliana Stratton, Democratic Senate nominee in Illinois
item.description: The lieutenant governor of Illinois, who won the Democratic Senate nomination, is a close ally of Gov. JB Pritzker. She has said ICE should be abolished.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton of Illinois speaking to residents at Montclair Senior Residences of Calumet Heights in Chicago last week.

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
